rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 11, 2011, line 9, CROSS-DEFENDANT JOSEPH UDOVCH DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST - 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION. CROSS-COMPLAINANT TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING APPLICATION OF THE DOCTRINE OF EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL. DEMURRER TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. ANY CLAIMS ABOUT PROPERTY SHOULD BE RAISED IN THE FAMILY COURT CASE. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CAMBRA VS. HERTZ EQUIPMENT RENTAL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 11, 2011, line 12, DEFENDANT HERTZ EQUIPMENT RENTAL CORPORATION, THE HERTZ CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S FEHA CLAIMS (1ST, 3RD, 5-6TH CAUSES OF ACTION) ARE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. PLAINTIFF'S INITIAL DFEH COMPLAINT WAS FILED, AND THE "RIGHT TO SUE" LETTER ISSUED OVER ONE YEAR PRIOR TO THE FILING OF THIS ACTION. THE ADDITION OF INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT, BRUCE WALKER, DOES NOT TOLL THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BECAUSE HE TOO WAS MENTIONED SPECIFICALLY IN THE DFEH COMPLAINT. SAAVEDRA V. ORANGE COUNTY CONSOLIDATION TRANSP. SER. AGENCY (1992) 11 CAL.APP.4TH 824, 826-28. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO CONVINCING ARGUMENT WHY THE DOCTRINE OF EQUITABLE TOLLING SHOULD APPLY. =(302/LMG)
